summary: This is a brief personal update about the author's visit to HCSSiM (Hampshire College Summer Studies in Mathematics) for Yellow Pig Day celebrations. She describes helping teach a workshop on Euler characteristics of planar graphs and counting Platonic solids using stereographic projection. The post captures a moment of mathematical community and education, mentioning plans to play Nim with students later.
key_points: Author visited HCSSiM for Yellow Pig Day, a quirky math camp tradition
Helped teach workshop on Euler characteristic and Platonic solids using stereographic projection
Successfully covered duals in addition to planned material
Plans to play mathematical games (Nim) with students at evening activity
money_quotes: I can never remember which one is the icosahedron.
key_insight: Mathematical community and playful learning thrive at summer programs where even experts admit to forgetting which solid is which.
